ops.title: User rejected hand-enumerated per-tool sets in hook logic — derive classification structurally from names
ops.body:
Reviewing the compact-restore plan, the user rejected the explicit `CREATE_TOOLS = {note_add, doc_add, …}` enumeration on sight ("dont do this").

**Why:** A hand-curated per-tool list is another place every new cc-notes command must be threaded (on top of [[cc-notes-new-commands-thread-into-hooks-skill]]) and silently misclassifies when forgotten. Same spirit as [[prefers-best-effort-over-locking]]: simple uniform rules over maintained machinery.

**How to apply:** When hook logic must classify cc-notes tools/commands, derive the class from the name's structure (token count + small verb vocabularies like `{add, open}`, `{show}`, `{rm}`, list-verbs) applied over the existing canonical set (`CC_NOTES_TOOLS` in the hooks pack), so a new `<kind>_add`/`<kind>_rm`/`*_show` classifies itself. Enumerations are acceptable only for genuinely irregular cases that carry no structure.
